reason: Undeclared dependencies on nodes 1.3 and 1.1. Node 1.4 declares only 'Uses node 1.2', but the symbols it reasons about are introduced elsewhere: (a) sgn(S):=S R and P:=(I+sgn(S))/2 are first DEFINED in node 1.3 ('Set sgn(S):=S R'; 'With P:=(I+sgn(S))/2'); without node 1.3 the symbol P -- whose value P(I)=I is the node's entire claim -- is undefined, so the node is not self-contained. (b) S:=2Phi-I originates in node 1.1; 1.4's use of S(I) and sgn(S)=S R inherits a dependency on 1.1's definition. This is the SAME defect the proof already corrected on a sibling: the resolved round-1 challenge ch-d274bd833965372d forced node 1.2 to declare its undeclared dependency on node 1.1. Sibling nodes 1.3 ('Uses nodes 1.1.2, 1.2'), 1.5 (cites 1.1, 1.1.1, 1.1.2, 1.2) and 1.7 (cites node 1.5) all declare their definitional dependencies; node 1.4 must likewise declare 1.3 (for sgn(S),P) and 1.1 (for S). SEVERITY minor: the mathematics is fully valid and the prior binomial-coefficient provenance gap (ch-7b16283ef4ec30f2) is genuinely resolved by the revision -- a_0=f(x0)=f(1)=1 is now correctly grounded in GT-funcalc/Kitaev approximate_algebras.tex:503 (Taylor center value), not in an uncited binomial value; this is a self-containment/citation defect, not a correctness error.
